findings.evidence: `rg -n "principalFunctionClassEquivPrincipalSubgroup|principalFunctionClassDivisor"` shows only the `mk` lemma at lines 108-113 and the divisor map/API at lines 117-133; there is no arbitrary-`q` bridge between the two public maps.
findings.fix: Add a characteristic lemma such as `@[simp] lemma coe_principalFunctionClassEquivPrincipalSubgroup (q) : ((S.principalFunctionClassEquivPrincipalSubgroup q : S.principalSubgroup) : WeilDivisor X) = S.principalFunctionClassDivisor q`, and optionally `principalFunctionClassDivisor_mem_principalSubgroup`/range equality derived from it.
findings.issue: `principalFunctionClassDivisor` is not related to `principalFunctionClassEquivPrincipalSubgroup` except on representatives; for an arbitrary `q : S.PrincipalFunctionClass`, the API has no lemma that the divisor map is the coercion of the subgroup equivalence, nor that its value lies in `S.principalSubgroup`.

summary: The new quotient and subgroup equivalence expose two parallel public maps but do not provide the characteristic lemma identifying them on arbitrary quotient classes, so downstream users cannot move between the bundled principal subgroup API and the induced map into Weil divisors without quotient induction/unfolding.
